what_it_does: Acceptance test file that exercises build_instruction from runtime.implement to verify it names governing module AGENTS.md files derived from scope paths, while preserving the root constitution line and other prompt blocks.
observations: Sets up repo root and COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment before importing build_instruction and STANDARDS_BLOCK.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and increments PASS.
Tests the 'HOP' case: two scope files in runtime/ and store/ cause both runtime/AGENTS.md and store/AGENTS.md to appear in the prompt.
Tests the 'WALK-UP' case: canvas/app/src/App.tsx resolves to canvas/AGENTS.md because intermediate dirs lack AGENTS.md.
Tests DEDUPE: two runtime files produce exactly one runtime/AGENTS.md mention.
Tests DIR-SCOPE: a bare module directory scope 'runtime/' names runtime/AGENTS.md.
Tests BACKWARD-COMPAT: empty scope and no-payload decisions still compose, keep the root three line, and name no module AGENTS.md.
Tests FAIL-LOUD-LEGIBLE: a top-level file like MAP.md composes without crash and keeps the root three line.
Tests PRESERVE: rich X4 context (address, symbols, context bundle) remains present alongside the new module constitution line.
standouts: The test is framed as a 'Convergence X15' acceptance test with a detailed docstring stating the principle, fix, and proof model.
It asserts fixture preconditions (existence/non-existence of specific AGENTS.md files) before exercising behavior.
It checks both presence and absence of strings, including exact count of occurrences for deduplication.
conventions: Uses uppercase scenario constants (HOP, NESTED, DEDUP, etc.) for test inputs.
Uses a shared check() helper with global PASS counter rather than a unittest/pytest class.
Imports implementation under test via sys.path manipulation and environment flag.
Tests string-formatter behavior directly without launching subprocesses or network calls.
concerns: The test depends on specific fixture files (runtime/AGENTS.md, store/AGENTS.md, canvas/AGENTS.md) and on canvas/app/src/ NOT having an AGENTS.md; if those fixtures change, the test breaks.
String-based assertions are sensitive to prompt wording changes in build_instruction, making the test brittle against unrelated formatting tweaks.
The global PASS counter and custom check() helper provide no built-in test discovery or reporting beyond printed 'ok' lines.
notes: A string-level acceptance test that verifies build_instruction adds governing module constitution paths next to the root three, without altering existing prompt content.
questions: What is the exact format of the added module-constitution line produced by build_instruction?
Does build_instruction normalize trailing slashes in directory scope entries before walking parents?
How does build_instruction handle scope paths that are absolute or outside the repo root?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to verify X15's constitution-hop behavior in build_instruction, and the file does exactly that through a series of string-presence/absence checks.
apparent_themes: Pure function testing without side effects
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock in a contract: when a decision names files under a module, the generated instruction must surface that module's AGENTS.md alongside the root constitutions, preserving everything else.
novelty: It treats prompt constitution naming as a testable, versioned convergence (X15) with explicit proof cases (HOP, WALK-UP, DEDUPE, DIR-SCOPE, BACKWARD-COMPAT, FAIL-LOUD-LEGIBLE, PRESERVE).
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that documents and guards the expected behavior of build_instruction's module-constitution naming feature, including edge cases for nested scopes, directory scopes, empty scopes, and coexistence with X4 rich context.
summary_for_embedding: tests/conv_constitution_acceptance.py is an acceptance test that verifies build_instruction derives and names governing module AGENTS.md files from scope paths while preserving the root constitution line and existing prompt blocks.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to acceptance-test the X15 constitution-hop feature in build_instruction and prevent regressions in backward compatibility and prompt preservation.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that checks a condition, increments a global pass counter, and prints an 'ok' message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a lightweight test assertion function used throughout the file. It asserts the supplied condition with a FAIL label on failure, otherwise increments the global PASS counter and prints a success line. It replaces a traditional unittest assertion style with a simple imperative helper.
